name: MITRE.LIN.T1016.RULE System Network Configuration Discovery
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-w /etc/hosts -p r -k siem-network-discovery
-w /etc/sysconfig/network -p r -k siem-network-discovery
-w /etc/network/ -p r -k siem-network-discovery
-a always,exit -F dir=/etc/NetworkManager/ -F perm=r -k siem-network-discovery
-w /etc/sysconfig/network -p r -k siem-network-discovery
-w /etc/netplan/ -p r -k siem-network-discovery

-w /usr/bin/ip -p x -k siem-network-discovery
-w /usr/sbin/ifconfig -p x -k siem-network-discovery
-w /usr/bin/nmcli -p x -k siem-network-discovery
-w /usr/sbin/route -p x -k siem-network-discovery
-w /usr/sbin/arp -p x -k siem-network-discovery

Set correct path for your linux distro (check 'whereis' command)

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-network-discovery
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1016.RULE System Network Configuration Discovery on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-network-discovery
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1016.RULE System Network Configuration Discovery

Event description: Adversaries may use the information from System Network Configuration Discovery during automated discovery to shape follow-on behaviors, including whether or not the adversary fully infects the target and/or attempts specific actions.

See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1016
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
